label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking why the author mentioned Zhang Wei's example. The options are A to D. Let me read through the passage again to make sure I understand the context. The passage starts by talking about how writers now can reach audiences online instead of going through publishers. It tells the story of Bao Jingjing, who started writing online after a breakup, became popular, and her work was adapted into a movie. Then it says that her story is one of many internet novels leading to deals. Then the author introduces Zhang Wei, known as Tangjiasanshao, who earned a lot of money and was on a Forbes list. The passage notes that he's at the top, but most online writers are unknown. However, he says others can succeed if they work hard enough. The question is about why the author mentioned Zhang Wei. Let me look at the options again: A) introduce another online writer to the readers B) show the example of another online writer's success C) encourage readers to become online writers D) express his admiration for online writer Zhang Wei The key here is to see the purpose of mentioning Zhang Wei. The passage already used Bao's story as an example of online success. Then Zhang Wei is presented as another example with even greater success (177 million yuan, on Forbes). The author also mentions that while he's at the top, others can succeed too if they work hard. So the author is using Zhang Wei as another example to support the point that online writers can achieve success, which complements Bao's story. So option B says "show the example of another online writer's success". That seems accurate because the author is providing another case to illustrate that online success is possible. Option C is about encouraging readers to become writers, but the passage doesn't explicitly call on readers to do that—it's more about showing that
